chapter_goal: Make the analytic, sheaf-theoretic, topological, and Kahler foundations inspectable as reusable machinery.

visual_sequence.concept: Cauchy formula and Weierstrass local finite cover
visual_sequence.inspection_target: contour samples recover the interior value while the square map shows the branch point
visual_sequence.invariant: Cauchy integral recovers f(0) within tolerance
visual_sequence.representation: sampled contour plus branched cover diagram

visual_sequence.concept: Analytic subvariety, singular point, and tangent cone
visual_sequence.inspection_target: compare the cusp with the tangent cone selected by the lowest degree term
visual_sequence.invariant: lowest homogeneous degree equals the tangent-cone multiplicity used in the check
visual_sequence.representation: level-set slice with leading homogeneous tangent cone

visual_sequence.concept: Sheaf gluing, de Rham/Dolbeault splitting, and proof dependencies
visual_sequence.inspection_target: local sections glue only when overlap differences vanish as a coboundary
visual_sequence.invariant: boundary squared and dbar squared are recorded as zero

visual_sequence.concept: Harmonic theory, Hodge decomposition, and Lefschetz ladder on a Kahler surface
visual_sequence.inspection_target: Hodge symmetry and Lefschetz pairing are visible by bidegree
visual_sequence.invariant: h^(p,q)=h^(q,p) and Lefschetz pairs have matching dimensions
